title: Find the hypotenuse — integer result
problem: A right-angled triangle has legs of length $3$ cm and $4$ cm. Find the length of the hypotenuse.

steps.instruction: Take the square root to find $c$.

title: Find the hypotenuse — decimal result
problem: A right-angled triangle has legs of length $5$ cm and $8$ cm. Find the length of the hypotenuse, rounded to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Take the square root. The result is not a whole number, so round to 2 decimal places.

title: Find the hypotenuse — multi-step problem
problem: A square has area $36$ cm². Find the length of its diagonal, rounded to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Find the side length of the square from its area.
The diagonal of a square divides it into two right-angled triangles, each with legs equal to the side length. Apply Pythagoras' theorem.
Take the square root and round to 2 decimal places.
